name: Billy Rose
description: American impresario, theatrical showman and lyricist (1899–1966)
abstract: Billy Rose (born William Samuel Rosenberg; September 6, 1899 – February 10, 1966) was an American impresario, theatrical showman, lyricist and columnist. For years both before and after World War II, Billy Rose was a major force in entertainment, with shows such as Billy Rose's Crazy Quilt (1931), Jumbo (1935), Billy Rose's Aquacade (193…

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Kirk got his break with Billy Rose, didn't he? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: We played 'Casa Mañana' just before we went to do 'Cabin in the Sky.' ... No this is a cabaret for Billy Rose. He had the 'Casa Mañana' restaurant. We played there for him for about six weeks.

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Rose called Barney Josephson and asked him to have me come up to the Diamond Horseshoe... So he says, 'Sam, now I'll tell you. I don't care what happens, you got the job.'

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Rose really loved music, and he surrounded himself with some of the best music that you could have at that time. All put together, it didn't mesh or blend as a show, but he did have -- he presented Benny Goodman and one show, Benny Goodman, Igor Stravinsky, and William Shuman and Cole Porter.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Tinney
about_quotes.evidence: she was way back in like the Hopkins people and all that, but her name was Isabel Jewel. And she came down to this Monroe’s Uptown House with one of the stars from Billy Rose’s Aquacade.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Tinney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Rose was the manager and he run it for the Kings Beer people... we played the first theater restaurant in New York. It was called the Casino de Paris.
